channel.title: Linux on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Linux on Pi Stack

item.title: Self-Hosted Network QoS: SQM Scripts vs CAKE vs TC-HTB Bandwidth Management (2026 Guide)
item.description: Network congestion ruins the experience for every user on a shared connection. Whether you are managing a home network with multiple streamers, an office with competing video calls, or a server handling mixed traffic types, Quality of Service (QoS) ensures critical traffic gets priority. This guide compares three leading open-source approaches to network traffic shaping: SQM Scripts, CAKE, and TC-HTB.

item.title: Lima vs Colima vs Podman Desktop — Self-Hosted Local Container Runtimes Guide 2026
item.description: Running containers locally on macOS or Linux requires more than just installing Docker. Apple’s move to Apple Silicon, combined with the shift away from Docker Desktop’s paid licensing for enterprises, has created demand for open-source local container runtimes. This guide compares three leading options: Lima, Colima, and Podman Desktop — examining how each handles local container orchestration, performance, and developer experience.

item.title: Cockpit vs Webmin vs Ajenti: Best Self-Hosted Server Management Web UI 2026
item.description: Managing Linux servers through SSH terminals works fine for a single machine. But once you are running five, ten, or fifty servers — or you manage servers for clients who refuse to use a command line — a web-based administration interface becomes essential.
